message:
fix(engine): order receipt signing before counter mutation in declassification pipeline

The pipeline previously incremented decision/allow/deny counters and
inserted emergency grants before calling emit_receipt() to sign the
receipt. If signing failed (e.g. bad key), the counters and grant map
were already mutated, leaving the pipeline in an inconsistent state
where it counted a decision that was never receipted.

Changes:
- Extract `build_signed_receipt()` from `emit_receipt()` so signing
  happens before any state mutation; only on success do counters
  increment and the receipt get recorded via `record_signed_receipt()`
- Apply the same build-then-commit pattern to the emergency grant path:
  build receipt first, then insert the grant and bump counters
- Add `latest_timestamp_ms` field to DeclassificationPipeline, updated
  by `record_signed_receipt()`, so `stats()` uses the last successfully
  receipted timestamp as its reference point for expiry calculations
- Add `stats_at(now_ms)` for explicit timestamp queries, and update
  `stats()` to delegate to it using `latest_timestamp_ms`
- Fix emergency grant active count to exclude expired grants by checking
  `is_expired(now_ms)` in the filter predicate
- Add tests: emergency grant expiry at boundary, later pipeline activity
  expires default stats, failed signing does not advance stats time

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
